reforms.description: The Canadian government proposed a programme for making assistance grants to support employment and mitigate the effects of US import taxes on certain industries.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The programme aims to support employment, which can enhance productivity and economic stability.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been put into action to support affected industries.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme is significant as it addresses employment impacts from external economic pressures.
reforms.source_quote: To support employment in Canada by mitigating the disruptive effects on those industries particularly hard hit by the US surcharge, the Canadian government proposed on 7th September a programme for making assistance grants.

reforms.description: The introduction of a 10 percent tax credit on purchases of new machinery and equipment produced in the United States aims to incentivize investment in domestic production capabilities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credit is designed to encourage investment in domestic production, which can lead to increased productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credit has been enacted and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The tax credit represents a significant incentive for businesses to invest in new equipment.
reforms.source_quote: a 10 percent tax credit on purchases of new machinery and equipment produced in the United States;
